translations.descricao: Low back pain, or lumbar pain, is one of the main causes of global disability, affecting millions of individuals. It represents a significant challenge in clinical protocols and screening for musculoskeletal disorders. An integrated approach between non-invasive treatment and multidisciplinary management is essential.
translations.sus.indicacao: Recommended for patients with acute, subacute, or chronic low back pain, prioritizing non-pharmacological approaches and occupational assessment in work-related cases.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults with persistent low back pain >3 months or recurrent episodes; workers exposed to ergonomic risk factors.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Non-invasive treatment recommended for acute, subacute, and chronic low back pain, with a focus on multimodal therapies.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Global point prevalence of 7.5% in adults; in Brazil, estimated at 18-30% for chronic low back pain.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global annual incidence of 5-10%; in Brazil, about 9% in working populations.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Low direct mortality, but contributes to 60.8 million years lived with disability globally; in Brazil, associated with occupational morbidity without significant mortality.
